name: Deep Chat
tagline: A customizable AI chat web component you can add to any site with one line of code
seoTitle: Deep Chat - Embeddable AI Chat Web Component
metaDescription: Deep Chat is a framework-agnostic chat web component you drop into any website and connect to OpenAI, Claude, your own backend, or an in-browser model.
overview: Deep Chat is a customizable AI chat component you can inject into a website with a single line of markup. It is built as a web component, so it works with plain HTML and all major UI frameworks and libraries, with a dedicated React wrapper available as a separate package.

It is aimed at developers who want a ready-made chat interface without building one from scratch. You can point it at your own backend service, connect directly to more than 20 popular AI APIs such as OpenAI and Claude from the browser, or host a model entirely in the browser with no server at all.

Within the chat-UI category, Deep Chat covers the front-end layer: it handles the message list, file and audio input, speech features, and styling, while you decide how requests reach a model. The direct-connection mode is meant for local prototyping and demos, since it exposes the API key to the browser; for production you connect through your own service or a proxy.
features: Connect to any backend API via the request property, or use interceptors and a handler function to shape requests
Direct in-browser connection to 20+ AI APIs including OpenAI and Claude for prototyping and demos
Host an LLM fully in the browser with the deep-chat-web-llm module and the webModel property
Send and receive files, capture photos via webcam, and record audio via microphone
Speech features: speech-to-text input, text-to-speech responses, and speech-to-speech communication
Markdown and custom-element rendering, avatars, names, browser storage for messages, and full styling control

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package, then add the deep-chat element to your markup. The exact syntax varies by framework.

steps.heading: Install
steps.body: Install the core package with npm. If you use React, install the React wrapper instead.

steps.heading: Install for React
steps.body: React projects should use the dedicated wrapper package.

steps.heading: Add the component
steps.body: Drop the element into your markup. On its own it renders the chat UI; add connection properties to wire it to a service.

steps.heading: Connect to a service
steps.body: Use the request property to point Deep Chat at an API that handles its request and response formats.

useCases: Add a chatbot to a marketing site or app that talks to your own backend service
Prototype against OpenAI or Claude directly in the browser before moving to a proxy for production
Ship a privacy-friendly assistant that runs an LLM entirely in the browser with no server
Build a support or documentation chat with file uploads, webcam capture, and speech input/output
